qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.title: Denial of Service vulnerability via ast.literal_eval permits resource exhaustion and server crashes
qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.advisory_match_reason: The finding describes a DoS vulnerability in pass-through endpoints via ast.literal_eval, not SSTI in the /prompts/test endpoint. Different vulnerable function, different root cause, different endpoint.

qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.title: SSRF vulnerability in pass-through endpoints permits unauthorized access to internal services
qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.advisory_match_reason: The finding describes SSRF in pass-through endpoint creation, not SSTI in the /prompts/test endpoint. Different vulnerability class, different endpoint, different root cause (missing URL validation vs. unsandboxed template rendering).

qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.title: Stored XSS vulnerability via SVG file upload permits attacker-controlled JavaScript execution in admin UI
qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.advisory_match_reason: The finding describes Stored XSS via SVG upload in /upload/logo and /get_image endpoints, not SSTI in the /prompts/test endpoint. Different vulnerability class, different endpoints, different root cause (missing file content validation vs. unsandboxed template rendering).

target_advisory_judgement.reason: No qualifying finding identifies the advisory-described SSTI vulnerability in the /prompts/test endpoint. The advisory describes server-side template injection where user-supplied prompt templates are rendered without sandboxing, enabling arbitrary code execution. The three qualifying findings describe: (1) DoS via ast.literal_eval in pass-through endpoints, (2) SSRF in pass-through endpoint creation, and (3) Stored XSS via SVG upload. None involve the /prompts/test endpoint, template rendering, or the same root cause.
evidence: Advisory describes SSTI in POST /prompts/test endpoint rendering user-supplied templates without sandboxing
dos-literal-eval: DoS via ast.literal_eval in pass-through endpoints - different endpoint, different vulnerability class
ssrf-pass-through-endpoints: SSRF via pass-through endpoint creation - different endpoint, different vulnerability class
stored-xss-svg-upload: Stored XSS via SVG upload - different endpoints, different vulnerability class
No finding mentions /prompts/test, template rendering, or sandbox bypass
